title.rendered: Aptio V UEFI Firmware from AMI Ready to Power AI Technology on NVIDIA Next-Gen AI Client PC Processors

content.rendered:
ATLANTA, GEORGIA – AMI®, the global leader in Dynamic Firmware for worldwide computing, is proud to announce that its Aptio® V UEFI Firmware is ready for use with NVIDIA’s next-gen AI client PC processors, following NVIDIA’s recent announcement at CES 2025. By leveraging AMI’s Aptio V UEFI Firmware as the reference design boot firmware solution, manufacturers can save time and cut development cost by choosing AMI for their AI-accelerated NVIDIA PCs.

Aptio V from AMI delivers the next generation of UEFI/BIOS to power up mission critical hardware components – processors, peripherals and interfaces – and enable easy integration of software packages such as operating systems, drivers, workloads and more. It also features native support for AMI’s Tektagon™ Hardware Root of Trust (HRoT) EC Firmware Solution for added security. Combining Aptio’s feature-rich platform package (IP) libraries with modular tools and architecture design ensures that the AI platform bring-up journey is efficient, cost-effective, dependable and stays on schedule.

“We are excited to announce our readiness to support NVIDIA’s pioneering next-generation AI client PC processors with our flagship Aptio V UEFI Firmware,” said Sanjoy Maity, CEO of AMI. “Our firmware enablement and CRB support for these new AI platforms from NVIDIA will ensure that customers continue to receive the best possible performance, reliability, and feature support from these new processors, setting a new standard for the industry.”

For more information about AMI’s products and services, please visit https://www.ami.com/.

Aptio® is a registered trademark of AMI US Holdings, Inc. All other trademarks and registered trademarks are the respective property of their owners.
